featuretablefilter 0.1.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering

    Filter samples based on ecological coverage estimators

    filter_by_coverage_estimator.Rd

    Filters samples based on Good's or Chao's coverage estimates, which measure the completeness of sampling rather than just absolute sequencing depth. This approach removes samples that are likely undersampled ecologically.

    filter_by_coverage_estimator(table, method = c("good", "chao"),
                              target_coverage = NULL, min_reads = 0)

    Arguments

    table

    A feature table (data.frame or matrix) with features as rows and samples as columns. First column should be feature IDs, remaining columns are sample counts.

    method

    Coverage estimation method: "good" for Good's coverage, "chao" for Chao's coverage.

    target_coverage

    Target coverage threshold (0-1). Samples below this will be filtered. Default is 0.95 for Good's, 0.90 for Chao's.

    min_reads

    Optional minimum absolute read count cutoff applied in addition to coverage. Useful as a safety floor. Default is 0 (no additional cutoff).

    Value

    A list containing:

    table

    Filtered feature table

    coverage_before

    Coverage estimates before filtering

    coverage_after

    Coverage estimates after filtering

    mean_coverage_before

    Mean coverage before filtering

    mean_coverage_after

    Mean coverage after filtering

    n_samples_before

    Number of samples before filtering

    n_samples_after

    Number of samples after filtering

    n_samples_filtered

    Number of samples removed

    method

    Method used ("good" or "chao")

    target_coverage

    Target coverage threshold used

    Examples

    # Filter using Good's coverage (keep samples with >= 95% coverage)
# result <- filter_by_coverage_estimator(table, method = "good", target_coverage = 0.95)

# Filter using Chao's coverage (keep samples with >= 90% coverage)
# result <- filter_by_coverage_estimator(table, method = "chao", target_coverage = 0.90)

    Contents
